Location.Name: New men's room in Castle Black
The tournament grounds in King's Landing
The Dungeons of the Red Keep
The room of Eddard in King's Landing
The prison cell in the Eyrie
A Certain Place in King's Landing
The prison cells of Robb's army
The Greyjoys' ship to the Iron Islands
Brienne and Catelyn on the road,fleeing

Session.dsp: Patrol north of the Wall encounters White Walkers
Will escapes south of the Wall and is captured by House Stark
In the Stark family training yard, Cassel reports the capture of the deserter
Eddard takes his sons to the execution ground to carry out the deserter’s sentence
On the way back, the Stark family discovers and adopts direwolves
Before the Iron Throne lies Jon Arryn’s corpse; Cersei and Jaime discuss Jon Arryn and their secret
Catelyn tells Eddard about the news of Arryn’s death and the king’s coming visit
Catelyn arranges the banquet
The Stark brothers have their hair cut to welcome the king
Bran climbs the castle wall, sees the king’s arrival, and gets scolded by his mother
Winterfell welcomes the king
Robert mourns Eddard’s sister, asks Ned to serve as Hand of the King, and proposes a marriage alliance
Jaime finds Tyrion in a brothel in Winterfell and tells him to attend the banquet
Daenerys becomes betrothed to Khal Drogo
Sansa discusses her marriage with her mother
In the banquet hall, House Stark holds a welcoming feast for the king
Jon meets his uncle Benjen at the training yard before the feast
After Benjen leaves, Tyrion speaks with Jon
Benjen joins the banquet
A letter arrives from Catelyn’s sister at the Eyrie
Daenerys and Khal Drogo’s wedding, with dragon eggs among the gifts
The king and Ned go out hunting
Bran witnesses Jaime and Cersei’s incest and is pushed from the tower
Khal Drogo’s host in Pentos: Viserys asks why Jorah Mormont is wanted.
In the Winterfell stables, Tyrion wakes up and tells Joffrey to offer condolences to the Starks.
At breakfast in the Lannister dining hall at Winterfell, Tyrion informs everyone that Bran has not died.
Cersei visits Bran’s sickbed.
Jon Snow meets Jaime while forging a sword for Arya.
Jon gives the sword to Arya and says farewell.
Jon says goodbye to Bran.
Eddard says farewell to Catelyn.
Everyone parts ways, setting out for King’s Landing and Castle Black.
On the road to King’s Landing, they discuss information about House Targaryen.
On the road to Castle Black, they meet the new recruit Samwell.
Bran is attacked.
Daenerys chats with her maidservants.
Jon and his companions reach the Wall.
Catelyn investigates at the tower and finds Lannister hair.
Catelyn gathers others, shares her findings, and decides to seek out Eddard.
The incident with Joffrey being bitten by a direwolf.
Bran awakens.
Eddard and Jaime discuss the past before the Iron Throne
Eddard’s first Small Council meeting ends in discord over financial problems
Cersei educates Joffrey
Eddard educates his daughters
Eddard discovers Arya’s sword and lets her keep it
Robb visits the awakened Bran
Catelyn arrives in King’s Landing; Littlefinger takes her to his brothel, where she discusses the dagger’s ownership with Varys
Mormont and Tyrion watch Jon training and receive news from Winterfell
Eddard receives the news from Winterfell
Petyr takes Eddard to meet Catelyn
Jon is beaten by the trainees, rescued and advised by Tyrion, who tells him Bran has awakened
Cersei and Jaime learn of Bran’s awakening and discuss countermeasures
Eddard bids farewell to Catelyn
Robert drinks and reminisces with Barristan Selmy
Daenerys clashes with Viserys
Benjen tells Jon he cannot become a ranger
Benjen rebukes Tyrion for his attitude toward the Night’s Watch
Jorah learns from Irri that Daenerys is pregnant and leaves the group to deliver the news
Jon, influenced by Tyrion, begins to change his attitude toward his companions
Mormont asks Tyrion to seek more support for the Night’s Watch in King’s Landing
Daenerys and Khal Drogo discuss the baby’s gender
Jon and Tyrion say farewell atop the Wall
Arya begins her swordsmanship training
Bran dreams of the three-eyed raven; Theon comes to tell him there are visitors
On his way back to King’s Landing, Tyrion visits Bran
Before leaving, Tyrion mocks Greyjoy
Samwell arrives at Castle Black
Arya and the others arrive at Vaes Dothrak
Sansa before the Iron Throne
The Small Council discusses security for the tourney
Eddard questions Pycelle about Jon Arryn’s final days
Eddard returns home, meets Arya, and speaks with her
During the night watch, Jon asks Sam about his family background
Littlefinger gives Ned a clue
Jory goes to find Jon Arryn’s former squire, Hugh
Following Littlefinger’s lead, Eddard finds Gendry
Jory Cassel delivers Ned’s letter to Robert but is stopped by Jaime
Jon threatens the others not to hurt Sam; Rast refuses
Jon and his friends threaten Rast at night
In the training yard, no one dares bully Sam anymore, angering the instructor
The Targaryen siblings clash
While Sam and Jon clean, Ser Alliser tells them they are about to graduate
Daenerys and Jorah discuss whether she can reclaim the Seven Kingdoms
The tourney takes place
Cersei visits Eddard
On the road, Tyrion encounters Catelyn and is captured
Eddard visits Ser Hugh, who died in the tourney.
Eddard goes to Robert’s pavilion.
The Knight of Flowers and the Mountain joust, the Hound intervenes to save him.
Catelyn’s party is attacked on the road, Tyrion saves her.
Luwin teaches Bran.
Theon goes to a brothel.
Varys warns Eddard about Jon Arryn’s death and Robert being in danger.
Arya chases cats.
Arya overhears Varys and Illyrio plotting in the dungeons of the Red Keep.
In the throne room, Varys and Petyr exchange barbs until Renly interrupts.
Eddard finds Arya and questions her about what she heard, but is interrupted by Yoren.
Catelyn and her companions arrive at the Eyrie.
At the Small Council, Eddard quarrels with Robert over the plan to assassinate Daenerys, and resigns as Hand.
Petyr visits Eddard, offering to take him to the last person Jon Arryn met.
Loras Tyrell encourages Renly to claim the throne.
Cersei talks with Robert about their past relationship.
Petyr leads Eddard to another of Robert’s bastards; they encounter Jaime, and Eddard is injured.
Robert and Cersei visit the recovering Eddard, urging reconciliation between their houses.
Daenerys places the dragon eggs on the fire, realizing she cannot be burned.
Bran rides a horse and is attacked by wildlings.
Tyrion attempts to bribe his jailer.
Arya continues her sword training, her father’s worries making her more determined.
Daenerys completes the ritual, while Jorah prevents Viserys from stealing the dragon eggs.
Tyrion tells Mord to pass a message that he is willing to confess.
At the Eyrie, Tyrion demands trial by combat, and Bronn secures his victory.
Robert and his companions go hunting.
Eddard, acting as Hand of the King, judges Gregor Clegane’s crimes.
Joffrey seeks Sansa’s forgiveness.
Theon encounters a prostitute leaving the North.
Eddard prepares to send his daughters home but suddenly realizes the truth about Joffrey’s parentage.
Khal Drogo kills the troublesome Viserys by pouring molten gold over his head.
Tywin and Jaime discuss countermeasures after being summoned to King’s Landing
Eddard confronts Cersei
Luwin inquires about the reason for sending the captives south
Jon Snow and Samwell spot Benjen’s horse returning while on watch at the Wall
Robert, wounded, entrusts the realm to Eddard
In his talk with Daenerys, Khal Drogo shows little interest in the continent of Westeros
Daenerys is poisoned but saved by Jorah
Jon and his group of recruits are assigned duties; Jon becomes Lord Mormont’s personal steward
Renly approaches Eddard, seeking his support to claim the throne
After sending word to Stannis, Eddard seeks Petyr’s help
Jon and Sam swear their oaths before the heart tree, and Ghost finds a severed hand
Khal Drogo vows to conquer Westeros
Conflict erupts before the Iron Throne; Eddard is arrested
Arya encounters Lannister soldiers during her sword lesson, escapes under Syrio’s cover, and kills a boy.
Sansa is held under Sandor’s control.
Varys visits Ned in the dungeon.
At Castle Black, they discuss the corpses found.
Mormont receives a letter from King’s Landing and summons Jon for a talk.
Cersei threatens Sansa into writing a letter urging her mother and brother to surrender.
Robb receives Sansa’s letter and prepares to raise his banners to rescue his father.
Lysa is asked to send troops by her sister but refuses.
Tyrion is ambushed but persuades his attackers to fight for him.
Ser Alliser provokes Jon, leading to Jon being confined.
At night, the corpses rise and attack Mormont; Jon, awakened by Ghost, saves him.
Daenerys rescues Mirri Maz Duur, leading Drogo to be challenged—he kills Mago but is wounded.
Robb summons the northern houses to war; Greatjon Umber loses fingers in a quarrel.
Robb says farewell to Bran.
Osha speaks with Bran about the old gods and beyond the Wall.
During the burning of corpses, Sam tells what he knows about the White Walkers.
Catelyn reunites with Robb.
Tyrion arrives at Tywin’s camp.
In Robb’s tent, they capture and release a scout.
At the Iron Throne, Barristan Selmy is dismissed; Sansa pleads for her father, but Joffrey demands Ned confess.
Varys once again tries to persuade Eddard to surrender.
Outside the Twin Rivers, the northern lords discuss their strategy, and Catelyn volunteers to go to the Twins for negotiations.
Catelyn arrives at the Twins to persuade Walder Frey to let them pass.
Jeor Mormont gives Longclaw to Jon Snow.
Jon learns from Sam that Robb has raised his banners for war.
Catelyn returns to camp and informs Robb of Walder’s conditions, which Robb accepts.
Aemon Targaryen calls Jon to speak with him and reveals his true lineage.
Drogo falls from his horse.
Tywin orders Tyrion and his men to lead the vanguard in tomorrow’s battle.
Tyrion returns to find Shae brought by Bronn and Dorellon.
Daenerys asks Mirri Maz Duur to use her magic to save Drogo.
Tyrion fights in battle, and the Lannister army wins.
Robb uses two thousand men as a decoy, defeats Jaime’s forces, and captures Jaime.
Eddard confesses at the Great Sept of Baelor and is executed.
Bran and Rickon both dream of their father and go to the crypts to look for him.
Winterfell receives news of Eddard’s death.
Robb’s army receives news of Eddard’s death.
Joffrey publicly executes a singer.
Joffrey forces Sansa to look at her father’s severed head.
The northern lords proclaim Robb as King in the North.
Catelyn questions Jaime about Bran’s fall.
Cersei learns that Jaime has been captured.
Tywin learns of Jaime’s capture and orders Tyrion to serve as Hand of the King, forbidding him to bring his woman.
Daenerys wakes to find Drogo in a vegetative state, and her people gone.
Tyrion returns to his tent and decides to take Shae with him.
Jon attempts to ride south to join Robb but is persuaded to return by his brothers.
Daenerys kills the unresponsive Drogo.
Pycelle recounts his service to past kings.
Joffrey summons Petyr and Varys.
Yoren takes Arya from King’s Landing to the Wall, traveling with several other children.
The Night’s Watch sets out beyond the Wall to investigate.
Daenerys burns Drogo’s body along with Mirri Maz Duur and walks into the flames with her dragon eggs, emerging reborn with dragons.
